name: Attleboro M E
description_short: Attleboro Municipal Employees Federal Credit Union (AMEFCU) is a Massachusetts-based NCUA-insured credit union serving municipal employees and members since 1963, offering loans, credit cards, and savings accounts.
description_long:
Attleboro Municipal Employees Federal Credit Union was founded in June 1963 when a small group of Attleboro city employees met to discuss forming a municipal credit union. The organization was officially approved on December 17, 1963, and began operations in January 1964 under the leadership of Melvin M. Adams. The credit union has operated continuously for over 60 years, relocating to its current headquarters at 138 South Main Street in 2002 under President Burdon Blanchard's leadership. The institution has maintained its original mission of providing members with convenient savings options and low-interest loan products.

AMEFCU offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including auto loans (APR as low as 5.25%), personal loans (APR as low as 11.00%), home equity loans (APR as low as 6.00%), recreational vehicle loans (APR as low as 7.00%), and a Visa Platinum credit card (11.90% APY with no annual fee or balance transfer fees). The credit union also provides online banking services, account-secured loans, and share savings accounts. Their loan products are competitively priced and marketed specifically for auto financing, home equity access, and personal borrowing needs.

AMEFCU distinguishes itself as a member-owned, not-for-profit institution with deep roots in the Attleboro municipal employee community. The credit union maintains governance through a board of directors and emphasizes peer-to-peer financial service delivery. Their longevity and stable leadership under charter members demonstrates organizational commitment to member welfare. The no-fee balance transfer credit card and competitive auto loan rates position them favorably among regional credit unions.

As a credit union, AMEFCU serves a specific membership base tied to Attleboro municipal employment, which may limit accessibility compared to banks open to the general public. While their rates are competitive, consumers should compare their offerings against other regional credit unions and banks. The website provides limited detail on membership eligibility requirements, account minimums, or fee structures beyond what is advertised for specific products.
pros: No annual fee on Visa Platinum credit card with no balance transfer fees
Competitive auto loan rates starting at 5.25% APR
Home equity loans available at rates as low as 6.00% APR
Online banking available for remote account management
Member-owned, not-for-profit structure returns profits to members
Established 60+ year track record with stable leadership continuity
Recreational vehicle financing option (7.00% APR) less common among smaller institutions
cons: Membership eligibility likely restricted to Attleboro municipal employees, limiting accessibility
Limited disclosure on website regarding account minimums, membership fees, or eligibility requirements
Personal loan rates starting at 11.00% APR are higher than auto loan rates, reflecting credit risk assessment
Website lacks detailed comparison tools or rate calculators for prospective borrowers
No mention of mobile app functionality despite promoting online banking
best_for: Attleboro municipal employees seeking member-owned financial services with competitive rates
Auto buyers looking for rates below 6% APR from a community-based credit union
Homeowners interested in home equity financing from an established local institution
Long-term savers seeking NCUA-insured share accounts with peer governance
diagnosis: AMEFCU is best suited for Attleboro municipal employees or eligible members seeking competitive loan rates and member-owned financial governance. The primary caveat is that membership eligibility is restricted to specific municipal employee groups, making this institution inaccessible to the general public despite their competitive product offerings.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.

meta_description: Attleboro M E — NCUA-insured credit union in Attleboro, MA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
